prompt: We turned on colored log levels in ourctl last sprint and the pager immediately started wrapping garbage — a line like ESC[31mERROR...ESC[0m gets chopped mid-escape so the terminal renders '[31mERRO' literally, and colored lines wrap way early because pager.py counts the escape bytes as if they took up columns. Please make the pager ANSI-aware. What I need in pager.py: a visible_width(s) helper that returns the on-screen column count ignoring CSI escape sequences (ESC then '[' then parameter bytes then a final letter — SGR color/bold/reset is all we emit, including multi-parameter ones like ESC[38;5;196m); wrap_line must break on VISIBLE columns only and must never, ever split inside an escape sequence — a sequence sticks to the character it styles, so when the break falls right where a color starts, the color code begins the next segment rather than dangling at the end of the previous one, and trailing codes like a reset stay on the last segment. A line that is only escape codes wraps to a single segment. A truncated sequence at end-of-string (our log tail does that sometimes) counts as zero width and must not crash or loop forever. paginate/page_count/render_page should pick all this up via wrap_line. Plain uncolored text has to wrap and paginate exactly as before — pixel for pixel, the existing tests pin it. Run python3 test_pager.py for the contract and keep your hands off the test file.
